page: Amazon Web Services r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The r6a.xlarge demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, alongside average single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores. However, multi-core performance is weaker, as evidenced by lower scores in multi-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation tests. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached and uncached reads and writes also fall into the lower performance tier, which impacts throughput-heavy operations.

This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense parallel CPU processing or high local storage I/O. Given the 32 GB RAM capacity paired with moderate CPU capabilities, the instance is suited for medium-sized databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. It is less optimal for high-throughput web serving, large-scale parallel compilation, or complex multi-threaded data processing due to its lower multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark results.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, delivering an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. Powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 2 physical cores, the instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, but multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. Consequently, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications with moderate compute requirements. It is best suited for workloads such as caching layers, relational databases, and memory-bound enterprise applications that do not demand high parallel processing power.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for memory-intensive workloads.

Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, offering 8 GB of RAM per vCPU.
